Interior of a large wood-paneled conference room in the White House, Washington D.C., February 1942. A long mahogany table is surrounded by five men: two U.S. Army generals in olive drab dress uniforms with peaked caps and medal ribbons leaning forward urgently, two senior male civilians in dark wool suits and ties, and a mid-60s man in a dark suit with wire-rimmed glasses seated at the head of the table. A large paper map of the American West Coast is spread across the table surface. Brass desk lamps cast warm amber light. Heavy drapes are half-drawn. The mood is tense and pressured. Mid-morning interior lighting.
